description: Implement the /hex-orient skill per ADR-2604170905. The skill provides on-demand agent onboarding — a single trigger that loads hex's hard rules + a curated subset of docs/reference/ (README, components, lessons) so fresh sessions can get grounded without reading everything. This fills the gap left by ADR-2604170900 where CLAUDE.md became a terse skill-map kernel. Non-goals: no auto-load at session start (would reintroduce the context tax); no duplication of CLAUDE.md rules verbatim (skill summarises and points back to CLAUDE.md as authoritative).

done: docs/reference/ tree created with 9 files including README.md, components.md, lessons.md

tasks.title: Create hex-cli/assets/skills/hex-orient.md with YAML frontmatter matching existing skills (name, description with trigger keywords). Description MUST include triggers: '/hex-orient', 'orient me', 'bring me up to speed', 'new to hex', 'agent onboarding'. Body sections: (1) What hex is — one paragraph, (2) Hard rules summary — 13 rules from CLAUDE.md in condensed form with explicit 'CLAUDE.md is authoritative' pointer, (3) System components tour — pull highlights from docs/reference/components.md (SpacetimeDB required, nexus bridges WASM↔OS, agent enforces architecture, dashboard is control plane), (4) Key lessons — 2-3 from docs/reference/lessons.md (tests-mirror-bugs, trace-all-consumers, build-gates-between-phases), (5) Where to go next — table mapping 'I want to…' → skill. No more than ~180 lines total.

Verify skill frontmatter parses correctly. Compare structure against hex-cli/assets/skills/hex-feature-dev/SKILL.md and hex-cli/assets/skills/hex-adr-create.md — name and description are required top-level keys. Decide: does /hex-orient use the flat form (hex-orient.md in assets/skills/) or the directory form (hex-orient/SKILL.md)? Flat form is simpler and matches hex-adr-* skills; use flat form.

phases.title: Register the skill in discovery surfaces

tasks.title: Add /hex-orient row to the skill-map table in CLAUDE.md (under '## Skill-first discovery'). Intent: 'Bring a fresh session up to speed on hex'. Insert alphabetically or at the end of the list — match existing row style.
Add /hex-orient row to the skills catalog in docs/reference/skills-and-agents.md. Include a one-line purpose: 'Agent onboarding — loads hard rules + components + lessons for fresh sessions'.

Verify the skill is baked into the binary via rust-embed. Check hex-cli/src/assets.rs (or wherever RustEmbed is declared) — confirm hex-cli/assets/skills/ is the embedded folder so hex-orient.md is picked up automatically with no additional registration needed.

Run cargo build --release for hex-cli and hex-nexus. Required because rust-embed bakes hex-cli/assets/* at compile time — edits to the skill file do not take effect in installed projects until the binary is rebuilt. This is a hard rule from CLAUDE.md (rule #3).

phases.title: Verify deployment into a target project

tasks.title: Choose an examples/ project (prefer a small one — todo-app or weather) and run the freshly-built `./target/release/hex init` in it. Confirm .claude/skills/hex-orient.md appears in the target, with content matching the source. If hex init does not deploy skills by default, consult hex-cli/src/commands/init.rs for the right flag or sub-step.

Smoke test: open a Claude Code session in the target project and verify /hex-orient appears in the skills list. If this cannot be scripted, leave a manual-verification note in the evidence and mark the task complete once P4.1 passes.
evidence: echo 'MANUAL: verify /hex-orient in Claude Code skill picker'

tasks.title: Commit the changes (hex-cli/assets/skills/hex-orient.md + CLAUDE.md + docs/reference/skills-and-agents.md) on the current branch with message 'feat(skills): add /hex-orient agent onboarding skill (ADR-2604170905)'. Push to origin.
evidence: git status --short | grep -v '^??' | wc -l | awk '{ if ($1 != 0) exit 1 }'

tasks.title: Flip ADR-2604170905 status from Proposed → Accepted, add 'Shipped in commit <hash>' to the Implementation section, and commit separately so the ADR status change is atomic and reviewable.

riskRegister.risk: Skill frontmatter schema has undocumented required fields (e.g., model, allowed_tools) — skill won't load
riskRegister.mitigation: P1.2 explicitly diffs against two known-good skills (hex-adr-create.md, hex-feature-dev/SKILL.md) before rebuild

riskRegister.risk: rust-embed caches stale assets if cargo build --release sees no Rust source changes
riskRegister.mitigation: If the binary still shows old assets after P3.1, touch a .rs file in hex-cli/src/ or run cargo clean -p hex-cli first

riskRegister.risk: hex init does not deploy newly-added skills into existing target projects (only on first init)
riskRegister.mitigation: P4 checks a fresh init. If existing projects need the skill, document a `hex refresh` or `hex init --force-skills` follow-up — out of scope for this workplan

riskRegister.risk: Skill body drifts from CLAUDE.md hard rules over time
riskRegister.mitigation: Skill explicitly labels itself as a summary and points to CLAUDE.md as authoritative. ADR-2604170905 flags adding a hex doctor check as future work — not in scope here

successCriteria: /hex-orient appears in Claude Code's skill picker when invoked in a hex-enabled project
Running the skill produces a concise orientation (<200 lines of loaded context) covering identity, hard rules, components, key lessons, and next-skill pointers
ADR-2604170905 is marked Accepted with the commit hash populated
No regressions: bun test, cargo check --workspace, and hex analyze . all pass
mergeOrder: P1 (skill file) → P2 (register in discovery surfaces) — P1 must complete first so the registrations point at a real file
P2 → P3 (rebuild) — rebuild picks up both the new skill and any CLAUDE.md references
P3 → P4 (verify deployment) — must use the rebuilt binary
P4 → P5 (commit + flip ADR) — only commit after deployment verified
